L1.area: Templating and content rendering layer for timesheet and reporting features

L2.description: A cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists where user-supplied content in timesheet entries is converted from Markdown to HTML and rendered in the browser without proper sanitization. An attacker can inject malicious scripts through timesheet descriptions, which are then executed when other users view timesheets, exports, or invoices.

L3.area: Markdown-to-HTML rendering extension and timesheet description display templates
L3.description: A cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists where user-supplied timesheet description content is converted from Markdown to HTML with raw HTML tags permitted, and the resulting output is rendered in export, invoice, and listing views without proper escaping. An attacker can inject malicious scripts through timesheet descriptions that execute when other users view these pages.

ground_truth.reason: User-supplied timesheet descriptions are rendered as HTML without escaping, and the Markdown-to-HTML converter allows raw HTML pass-through, enabling script injection when other users view timesheets, exports, or invoices.
